headline: Survey at 22% completion with strong regional imbalance and clear expansion mandate.
collection_status: 299 of 1,350 responses collected (22.1% of target). Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 56% of data. Collection pace insufficient to meet target without acceleration; 10 regions active but Rukwa (5) and Manyara (4) severely underrepresented.
key_findings: Stakeholders decisively prioritize expansion: 110 votes for expanding KiuFunza (37% of priorities) vs. 56 for strengthening existing model, signaling appetite for scale.
Teacher voice dominates (146 of 299; 49%), but critical coverage gaps exist: only 7 Regional Coordinators and 2 REOs across 10 regions limits institutional insight.
Scale readiness concern: average 2.7/5 suggests stakeholders see significant barriers to growth despite high expansion demand; partner satisfaction strong at 4.4/5.
Feedback themes converge on three needs: sustained teacher incentives, government policy integration, and digital monitoring systems—actionable priorities for next phase.
flags: Rukwa and Manyara critically undercollected (<5 responses each); risk of regional blind spots affecting national strategy.
Scale readiness (2.7/5) substantially lags expansion demand (37% priority votes)—gap indicates resource, system, or capacity constraints not captured in current data.
Regional coordinator and education officer representation extremely low (9 of 299; 3%)—insufficient for validating district and regional feasibility assumptions.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection to Rukwa, Manyara, and undersampled districts in Singida and Shinyanga; simultaneously schedule structured interviews with 15–20 REOs, RCs, and DEOs to diagnose the scale readiness gap before expansion planning proceeds.
